Class WriteAndReviewAgent

java.lang.Object
com.embabel.template.agent.WriteAndReviewAgent
public class WriteAndReviewAgent extends Object
An Embabel agent that generates a creative story and then reviews it.

Embabel @Agent Annotation: The Agent annotation marks this class as an Embabel agent — a Spring stereotype that is automatically discovered via classpath scanning and registered with the AgentPlatform. The description field is critical: it is used by the platform to select this agent when processing user input in Closed mode (where the LLM chooses the best agent based on descriptions).

GOAP Planning: By default, agents use Goal Oriented Action Planning (GOAP) — a non-LLM AI algorithm that dynamically creates execution plans by analyzing the preconditions and effects (input/output types) of each Action method. The planner determines the optimal sequence of actions to achieve the declared AchievesGoal. This means:

  • The framework infers that craftStory must run before reviewStory because reviewStory requires a WriteAndReviewAgent.Story parameter.
  • Both actions require UserInput, which is provided as the initial input.
  • The plan is re-evaluated after each action (OODA loop), allowing adaptation.

Domain Model: The strongly-typed records WriteAndReviewAgent.Story and WriteAndReviewAgent.ReviewedStory form the domain model. They flow through the agent's actions as inputs and outputs, enabling compile-time safety and full IDE refactoring support — no "magic maps".

See Also:
  • Agent
  • Action
  • AchievesGoal

  • Constructor Details

    • WriteAndReviewAgent

      WriteAndReviewAgent(@Value("${storyWordCount:100}") int storyWordCount, @Value("${reviewWordCount:100}") int reviewWordCount)
      Constructor with Spring Value injection for configurable word counts.

      Because @Agent is a Spring stereotype, this class is a Spring bean. Spring injects constructor parameters, including @Value properties with defaults. This allows runtime configuration via application.properties/yml or command-line arguments (e.g., --storyWordCount=200).

      Parameters:
      storyWordCount - maximum word count for generated stories (default: 100)
      reviewWordCount - maximum word count for reviews (default: 100)

  • Method Details

    • reviewStory

      @AchievesGoal(description="The story has been crafted and reviewed by a book reviewer", tags={"creative-writing","story-review"}, examples={"Write and review a story about a robot learning to paint","Tell me a story about caterpillars and review it"}, export=) @Action(description="Review the generated story using a book reviewer persona") WriteAndReviewAgent.ReviewedStory reviewStory(com.embabel.agent.domain.io.UserInput userInput, WriteAndReviewAgent.Story story, com.embabel.agent.api.common.Ai ai)
      Reviews the generated story using a reviewer persona.

      @AchievesGoal: Marks this action as the terminal action — completing it means the agent's goal is achieved and execution ends. The description is used by the platform to match user intent to goals in Open mode. The tags and examples fields help with goal classification and selection.

      @Export: Exposes this goal as a remotely-callable tool named "writeAndReviewStory", enabling A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol integration and MCP server exposure with zero additional code.

      Action Parameters: The GOAP planner infers preconditions from method parameters: UserInput (provided at invocation) and Story (produced by craftStory). The Ai parameter is a special framework-injected context giving access to LLM operations.

      PromptRunner: ai.withAutoLlm() returns a PromptRunner configured with automatic model selection. The fluent API chains .withPromptContributor() to inject the reviewer persona into the system prompt, then .generateText() to produce a plain text review.

    • craftStory

      @Action(description="Craft a creative story based on user input") WriteAndReviewAgent.Story craftStory(com.embabel.agent.domain.io.UserInput userInput, com.embabel.agent.api.common.Ai ai)
      Crafts a creative story based on user input.

      @Action: Marks this method as an action the GOAP planner can include in its plan. The planner infers preconditions from the parameter types (UserInput) and postconditions from the return type (Story). No explicit pre/post annotations are needed when data flow defines the dependencies.

      LLM Configuration: Uses LlmOptions.withAutoLlm() with a higher temperature (0.7) for more creative output. Other options include:

      • LlmOptions.withModel("gpt-4") — select a specific model by name
      • ai.withLlmByRole("best") — select by configured role
      • ai.withDefaultLlm() — use the platform default model
      • ai.withFirstAvailableLlmOf("model-a", "model-b") — fallback chain

      createObject(): Unlike generateText(), this method deserializes the LLM response into a strongly-typed Story record. The framework handles JSON schema generation, prompt construction, and response parsing automatically.
